title: Black-Scholes Model: The Black-Scholes model can be used to value options on...
summary: Evaluate the Black-Scholes model's assumptions and limitations for option pricing.
prompt: The Black-Scholes model can be used to value options on any underlying asset.
solution: The Black-Scholes model is primarily designed for European options on non-dividend-paying stocks. It has specific assumptions that may not hold true for all types of options or underlying assets, particularly those with dividends or American-style options.
